domain: textile manufacturing (Jacquard loom punched-card pattern encoding, 1801)
specific_mechanism: Jacquard's loom stores a weaving pattern as a chain of cards; each card position controls a single warp thread for one weft pass (hole = raise, no hole = leave). The chain is fed in strict sequence — the card order IS the program; reorder the cards and the pattern collapses. Phase coherence between card position and shuttle pass is mechanical (the cards advance in lockstep with the beat-up).
llm_application: Treat decoder-token positions analogously to Jacquard card positions: at each generation step, a per-position 'card' is a sparse pattern over a vocabulary subset that selects which embedding sub-channels are active. The card's hole-pattern is determined by a small instruction-tokenizer that converts an instruction-prefix into a per-position activation mask. Generation 'coherence' is the requirement that the card chain stay phase-locked to the prefix — out-of-phase masks degrade pattern fidelity exactly as Jacquard miscards collapse the weave.
novelty_claim: Subword tokenization (BPE/WordPiece/Unigram), instruction-level tokenization (UniASM-style), and tokenization-effect-on-reasoning analyses (2601.14658 'Tokenizer Betrays Reasoning') are well-studied. None proposes a per-decoder-position card-pattern mask drawn from a small instruction-tokenizer that is phase-locked to the prefix — the lockstep coherence requirement is what is open.
